creator: timo.voelker@fh-muenster.de
text:
(In reply to Michael Tuexen from comment #2)

I cannot reproduce this in the same setup, because I don't have this router and ISP anymore that triggered the router to send IPv6 Prefixes with a lifetime of 0.

I found a pcap file from that time that contains such a Router Advertisement with IPv6 Prefixes with a lifetime of 0. I attached it.

I also found a protocol from that time. It contains steps to reproduce the issue. It seems that the router is not required for that. I'll check.
